what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block item that records a compact design synthesis for a collaborative annotation/comment architecture, listing three failure modes inherited from incumbent tools and the corresponding architectural countermeasures.
observations: YAML frontmatter carries id, board address, type=block, source=claude_code, state=current, title 'P11 · One-line for synthesis', author session, channel=dragnet-development, and a part_of link to item-64466290.
The body proposes anchoring annotations by redundant text-quote plus content-hash, re-resolved at read time and flagged when stale.
It proposes splitting user intent into comment/question versus change-request-with-accept-able-diff.
It proposes a durable batch draft-queue with a send-now escape hatch.
It states the tap-to-select / long-press-for-power gesture must be verified on real iOS devices to win the scroll/callout war.
It references Google Docs, Figma, Hypothesis, and GitHub as sources of the 'three scars'.
standouts: The 'three scars' synthesis: anchor-by-content-hash, intent split, and durable batch queue.
The explicit requirement for real-device iOS verification before trusting the gesture design.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, channel, links, timestamps, and history block.
Uses a Markdown heading and a single dense paragraph for the note body.
concerns: The title claims 'one-line' but the body is a multi-sentence paragraph.
The prescriptive advice is abstract and contains no concrete implementation or acceptance criteria within the file.
notes: A design-synthesis note that distills three anti-fragility rules for a comment/annotation substrate and warns that the mobile gesture UX must be validated on real iOS hardware.
questions: What does 'P11' denote and what is the sequence it belongs to?
What is the parent item-64466290 and how does this note relate to it?
What specific product or feature is this architecture for?
What exactly constitutes a 'send-now escape hatch' in the proposed UI?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly aligned: it is a concise synthesis, though the body is not literally one line.
intention_signals: To capture a portable, compact rule-set so the architecture avoids known data-integrity, intent, and friction pitfalls of existing collaboration tools.
novelty: It bundles three distinct anti-scar prescriptions—content-hash re-resolution, intent split, and durable batch queue—into a single synthesis, and couples them with an explicit real-device iOS verification requirement.
contribution: A named set of three design constraints and a real-device verification heuristic that a fused system could retain as portable requirements for annotation/comment UX.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design note titled 'P11 · One-line for synthesis' that distills three architectural safeguards against anchoring, intent, and batching failures found in existing collaboration tools, and insists on real-device iOS gesture verification.
intention_for_embedding: To record a compact design rule for building a comment/annotation substrate that avoids known scars of incumbent tools.
